title: Complete user-space inventory and lossless ingestion defined
supporting_sources: 1.0_VISION/Notebooklm/Architecture Context Matrix_ PostgreSQL-First LifeOS Framework - Table 1.csv
task_graph.source.csv rows POSTGRES-002 (user-space class enumeration) and POSTGRES-003 (control-plane collector)

scope_boundary.this_task_defines: Archive-first, read-only collectors and lossless ingestion contracts that inventory the entire user-space and import it into STAGING (transient redb buffer, then envctl-bridged PostgreSQL staging tables) without changing any source state.
scope_boundary.this_task_does_not: Run live ingestion, mutate any source, provision databases, write live rows, or perform the authority cutover (that is POSTGRES-010 under the STORE-001 verified-cutover invariant). No product execution is performed.
scope_boundary.no_source_mutation: All collectors are read-only. blocked_paths (live ingestion, source mutation, destructive scan, credential values, raw secrets, product implementation) are honored by construction.

store_authority_alignment.canonical_durable_owner: PostgreSQL + RuVector — the single canonical owner of durable relational + semantic macro-state (ACID / PITR, No-Sidecar native extension).
store_authority_alignment.transient_buffer: redb — intended terminal role is the high-frequency buffer / scratchpad / dedup cache; ingestion writes land here FIRST and are forwarded, never held as durable authority.
store_authority_alignment.forward_only_bridge: envctl — the one-directional bridge redb -> embed -> PostgreSQL and the projection engine PostgreSQL -> filesystem. envctl is NOT a second authority (AUTH-CLAIM-001 refuted); PostgreSQL is the destination authority.
store_authority_alignment.parser_path: nu_plugin CodeDB — Nushell AST parser that streams typed records into the redb buffer via MessagePack, per the blueprint Three Pillars.
store_authority_alignment.staging_is_pre_cutover: Ingestion targets STAGING tables under one SHA-256 content identity. No existing durable redb class (CodeDB source_blobs PGREDB-001, envctl migration_db PGREDB-002) is silently reclassified; any move off redb-durable happens only later via the verified BlobStore SHA-256 parity cutover (STORE-001 invariant 'Verified cutover').

ingestion_pipeline.model: Three Pillars of Ingestion (Architecture Blueprint sections 5 and Phase 1; NBSOURCE-030).

stages.action: Traverse the bounded user-space read-only; for code, parse source into ASTs (functions, structs, dependencies); for every other class, emit typed records. Stream via MessagePack (no JSON serialization bottleneck).
stages.grounding: Blueprint 'The Parser'/'Three Pillars'; NBSOURCE-030 INGEST-CLAIM-001; context matrix envctl+nu_plugin row.

stages.action: nu_plugin writes parsed blocks into the local redb staging buffer at low latency; BLAKE3 content hashing acts as the deduplication cache so identical blocks are never re-processed.
stages.grounding: Blueprint 'The Buffer'; redb dedup cache (blueprint section 4, BLAKE3); STORE-001 transient class; context matrix redb row 'Exact Key (BLAKE3 Hash for cache)'.

stages.action: envctl pulls blocks from the redb buffer, attaches provenance, computes/attaches SHA-256 content identity, and commits code + metadata + (where in scope) vectors into PostgreSQL STAGING tables. Forward-only; PostgreSQL is the durable destination.
stages.grounding: Blueprint 'The Bridge'/envctl; NBSOURCE-015 VECTORFLOW-CLAIM-004..007; STORE-001 sync_direction 'redb -> envctl(embed) -> PostgreSQL (forward-only)'.

ingestion_pipeline.archive_first: Before any pillar runs, a checksummed read-only archive of the source surface is recorded (PRESERVE-001 pattern: git bundle / file+byte counts / permissions / device+inode). The archive is the rollback and the reconstruction oracle; it is never modified.

global_invariants.archive_first_read_only: Collectors open sources read-only; no write, rename, chmod, or delete on any source path. The only writes are to the archive root and the staging buffer.
global_invariants.single_content_identity: Every collected entity is content-addressed by SHA-256 of its canonical bytes (BlobStore identity per ADOPT-001 / STORE-001). The redb dedup cache keys on BLAKE3 (blueprint). One entity => one identity across retries.
global_invariants.exactly_once: Staging upserts are keyed on the stable identity; a resume skip-set of already-committed identities plus BLAKE3 dedup make re-runs idempotent (no duplicate rows, no recomputed inference).
global_invariants.restartable_checkpoints: A per-class cursor and the resume skip-set are persisted in the envctl migration_db (targets, runs, run_events, approvals, checkpoints, rollbacks — PGREDB-002). An interrupted run resumes from the last committed checkpoint.
global_invariants.provenance_on_every_record: Each staged record carries: source_path, collector_id, collection_run_id, collected_at, content_sha256, metadata_sha256, and source lineage (git commit/PR where applicable, per ADOPT-001 provenance).
global_invariants.secret_exclusion: Credential VALUES and raw secrets are never collected. Only references/provenance are recorded (STORE-001 external-secret class: 'PostgreSQL stores references/provenance, never raw secrets; redb never holds secrets').
global_invariants.deterministic_ordering: Every class declares a total order so repeated collection and repeated projection are byte-identical (feeds POSTGRES-008 'repeated projections are byte-identical').
global_invariants.lossless_round_trip: For every class, staged records must reconstruct the archived source byte-for-byte (content), tuple-for-tuple (metadata), and in declared order — verified against the archive oracle before any authority claim.

user_space_classes.description: Regular file byte content across the bounded user-space (text and binary handled uniformly as opaque bytes at this class; code-specific parse is USI-C03, non-text specifics are USI-C08).

bounded_collector.surface: All regular files under the approved archive roots (workspace + user-data roots).
bounded_collector.bounds: Explicit root allow-list; symlinks recorded as edges (USI-C12), not followed across roots; max-depth and per-file byte cap for streaming (large blobs handed to USI-C08).
bounded_collector.method: Read-only open + streamed read; nu_plugin emits a typed file record.

user_space_classes.exclusion_policy: Skip blocked_paths, VCS internal object stores (captured once via USI-C11 history), build/exhaust dirs (node_modules, target, .nix exhaust), and any file whose bytes match a secret pattern (recorded as reference only, never bytes).
user_space_classes.stable_identity: SHA-256 of file bytes (content address) bound to the normalized logical path; identity = (content_sha256, logical_path).
user_space_classes.content_checksum: SHA-256 over the exact file bytes (BlobStore identity, ADOPT-001).
user_space_classes.metadata_checksum: SHA-256 over the canonical JSON of {size, mode, uid, gid, mtime, ctime, device, inode} (PRESERVE-001 metadata set).
user_space_classes.ordering_contract: Lexicographic by normalized logical path; ties broken by content_sha256.
user_space_classes.checkpoint: Per-run cursor = last committed logical_path; resume skip-set = committed content_sha256 set (envctl migration_db).
user_space_classes.exactly_once: Idempotent upsert keyed on (content_sha256, logical_path); BLAKE3 dedup cache skips unchanged bytes.
user_space_classes.failure_recovery: Resume from cursor; redb ACID re-open recovers the buffer; re-read of unchanged bytes from the read-only working tree fills gaps (PGREDB-001 pattern).
user_space_classes.lossless_reconstruction_test: envctl projects staged bytes to a scratch path; assert projected SHA-256 == archived content_sha256 and metadata tuple matches (exact-byte materialization, blobstore_parity).
user_space_classes.store_role: redb buffer (transient) -> envctl bridge -> PostgreSQL staging blob/rows; bytes durable in BlobStore under SHA-256.

user_space_classes.description: Directory tree structure and directory-level metadata; the containment skeleton of the user-space.

bounded_collector.surface: Every directory under the approved roots.
bounded_collector.bounds: Same root allow-list and max-depth as USI-C01; mount boundaries not crossed.
bounded_collector.method: Read-only directory walk emitting a typed directory record with ordered child identities.

user_space_classes.exclusion_policy: Skip blocked_paths and VCS internal directories except as captured by USI-C11; do not descend into excluded subtrees.
user_space_classes.stable_identity: Normalized logical directory path; identity = (logical_path, tree_position).
user_space_classes.content_checksum: Merkle-style SHA-256 over the ordered list of child identities (directories have no bytes; their content is ordered membership).
user_space_classes.metadata_checksum: SHA-256 over canonical {mode, uid, gid, mtime, ctime} of the directory node.
user_space_classes.ordering_contract: Depth-first, children in lexicographic order; parent recorded before children.
user_space_classes.checkpoint: Per-run cursor = last committed directory path; the tree is re-derivable, so the cursor is advisory.
user_space_classes.exactly_once: Idempotent upsert keyed on logical_path; the Merkle content_checksum detects membership drift between runs.
user_space_classes.failure_recovery: Re-walk is safe and cheap; membership recomputed deterministically from the read-only tree.
user_space_classes.lossless_reconstruction_test: Reconstruct the directory skeleton from staged records and assert the Merkle root over reconstructed children equals the archived Merkle root; feeds envctl module_path directory reconstruction (POSTGRES-008).
user_space_classes.store_role: redb buffer -> PostgreSQL staging directory table; canonical containment edges materialized in USI-C12.
grounding: Blueprint materialization 'module_path metadata to map the directory structure'

user_space_classes.description: Source code parsed into Abstract Syntax Trees: functions, structs, dependencies, and module_path metadata — the database-hosted-codebase substrate.

bounded_collector.surface: Source files (.rs, .toml, .nu, .ts, .vue, etc.) under the approved roots.
bounded_collector.bounds: Language-typed parse only; files that fail to parse are staged as raw USI-C01 bytes with a parse_failed flag (never dropped).
bounded_collector.method: Nushell parser / nu_plugin CodeDB produces AST blocks (functions/structs/deps) streamed via MessagePack into redb; module_path metadata attached.

user_space_classes.exclusion_policy: Skip generated/exhaust code (napi-rs, wasm-pack) per gitkb/meta isolation; skip vendored dependency trees; never embed secret literals.
user_space_classes.stable_identity: (module_path, symbol_kind, symbol_name) + SHA-256 of the raw_code block; module_path is the reconstruction key.
user_space_classes.content_checksum: SHA-256 over the raw_code block bytes of each AST fragment.
user_space_classes.metadata_checksum: SHA-256 over canonical {module_path, language, symbol_kind, symbol_name, dependency_refs, byte_span}.
user_space_classes.ordering_contract: File order (USI-C01 path order), then AST document order (byte_span ascending) within each file.
user_space_classes.checkpoint: Per-run cursor = (logical_path, byte_span); resume skip-set = committed raw_code SHA-256; envctl migration_db checkpoint.
user_space_classes.exactly_once: Idempotent upsert keyed on raw_code SHA-256 + module_path; BLAKE3 dedup skips re-parsing identical blocks (blueprint dedup cache).
user_space_classes.failure_recovery: Re-parse from the read-only source is deterministic; redb ACID recovers buffered blocks; envctl replay re-derives commit state (PGREDB-002).
user_space_classes.lossless_reconstruction_test: envctl concatenates raw_code blocks by module_path back into complete .rs/.toml/.nu files (blueprint 4-step materialization) and asserts reconstructed-file SHA-256 == archived file SHA-256 (byte-exact).
user_space_classes.store_role: nu_plugin -> redb buffer -> envctl embed+bridge -> PostgreSQL staging code rows (raw_code, module_path, dependency edges; embeddings deferred to POSTGRES-005).
grounding: Blueprint Three Pillars / Ingestion Pipeline (AST fragments, module_path)

user_space_classes.description: Configuration files and declared environment shape: toml/json/yaml, dotfiles, .meta.yaml, Cargo.toml, tsconfig, capability manifests.

bounded_collector.surface: Recognized configuration files under the approved roots.
bounded_collector.bounds: Parsed as typed key trees where possible; otherwise staged as raw bytes (USI-C01) with a config flag.
bounded_collector.method: Read-only parse to typed key/value tree; secret values redacted to references at collection time.

user_space_classes.exclusion_policy: Redact credential VALUES and raw secrets to a reference token (STORE-001 external-secret class); never stage secret material.
user_space_classes.stable_identity: (logical_path, config_format) + SHA-256 of the redacted canonical bytes.
user_space_classes.content_checksum: SHA-256 over the redacted canonical config bytes.
user_space_classes.metadata_checksum: SHA-256 over {logical_path, config_format, redaction_map_digest, size, mode}.
user_space_classes.ordering_contract: Logical path order; within a file, canonical key order of the format.
user_space_classes.checkpoint: Cursor = last committed config logical_path; skip-set = committed redacted-content SHA-256.
user_space_classes.exactly_once: Idempotent upsert keyed on (logical_path, redacted content_sha256).
user_space_classes.failure_recovery: Re-parse from read-only source; redaction is deterministic given the secret-pattern set.
user_space_classes.lossless_reconstruction_test: Reconstruct the redacted config file and assert redacted-bytes SHA-256 == archived redacted SHA-256; secret positions round-trip as reference tokens with no value loss beyond the intentional redaction (recorded in redaction_map).
user_space_classes.store_role: redb buffer -> PostgreSQL staging config table; secret references only.
grounding: STORE-001 external-secret class (references, never raw secrets)
task blocked_paths: credential values / raw secrets

user_space_classes.description: The control-plane tabular state: task_graph.source.csv, generated/*.json control artifacts, GitKB documents, North Star anchors, proof URIs.

bounded_collector.bounds: Read-only snapshot; this collector is the ingestion feeder for the POSTGRES-003 database-native control plane.
bounded_collector.method: Typed-row extraction keyed on primary identity (task_id / slug / usage_id).

user_space_classes.exclusion_policy: Skip ephemeral scratch and non-control artifacts; no secret material present in control tables (already reference-only).
user_space_classes.stable_identity: Primary key of the row (task_id, slug, usage_id, proof task_id) + SHA-256 of canonical row bytes.
user_space_classes.ordering_contract: Primary-key ascending within each source artifact; artifacts in declared load order.
user_space_classes.checkpoint: Cursor = (source_artifact, last primary_key); skip-set = committed row SHA-256.
user_space_classes.exactly_once: Idempotent upsert keyed on primary_key; deterministic projection back to CSV/JSON preserves the row (POSTGRES-003 deterministic projection contract).
user_space_classes.failure_recovery: Re-read from read-only control artifacts; rows are content-addressed so partial runs resume cleanly.
user_space_classes.lossless_reconstruction_test: Project staged rows back to CSV/JSON and assert byte-identical to the archived control artifact (POSTGRES-003 'CSV and documents become deterministic projections').
user_space_classes.store_role: redb buffer -> PostgreSQL staging control tables; becomes canonical under POSTGRES-003.
grounding: POSTGRES-003 gate (database-native control plane, deterministic projection)

user_space_classes.description: Message and correspondence histories: chat threads, AI conversation logs, and inter-agent messages present in the user-space.

bounded_collector.surface: Stored message logs and conversation archives under the approved roots.
bounded_collector.bounds: Archive-first snapshot of at-rest message stores only; no live capture of in-flight streams.
bounded_collector.method: Read-only extraction of normalized message records (thread, author, timestamp, body).

user_space_classes.exclusion_policy: Redact embedded secrets/tokens in message bodies to references; skip volatile live buffers (e.g., runtime aiMessages, which are excluded from persistence and are URL/session-ephemeral).
user_space_classes.stable_identity: (thread_id, author, sequence) + SHA-256 of the normalized message body.
user_space_classes.content_checksum: SHA-256 over the normalized message body bytes.
user_space_classes.metadata_checksum: SHA-256 over {thread_id, author, timestamp, sequence, channel}.
user_space_classes.ordering_contract: (thread_id ascending, monotonic timestamp, sequence) — total order per thread.
user_space_classes.checkpoint: Cursor = (thread_id, last sequence); skip-set = committed message SHA-256.
user_space_classes.exactly_once: Idempotent upsert keyed on (thread_id, sequence, content_sha256).
user_space_classes.failure_recovery: Resume per-thread from last committed sequence; at-rest stores are re-readable.
user_space_classes.lossless_reconstruction_test: Reconstruct each thread in order and assert per-message SHA-256 and thread ordering match the archived snapshot.
user_space_classes.store_role: redb buffer -> PostgreSQL staging communications table.

user_space_classes.description: At-rest application state: LifeOS persisted keys, the lifeos.db SQLite store, redb runtime state files, and envctl migration_db state.

bounded_collector.surface: $APPDATA/lifeos/* persisted state, lifeos.db, redb state files, envctl migration_db.
bounded_collector.bounds: Consistent read-only snapshot (copy-then-read of DB files) so live processes are never blocked or mutated.
bounded_collector.method: Snapshot + typed extraction of state keys and rows.

user_space_classes.exclusion_policy: Persist only whitelisted/meaningful state (per LIFEOS_PERSIST_KEYS discipline); exclude ephemeral keys (cmdkOpen, extraItems, pendingExpand); never stage secrets from keyring.
user_space_classes.stable_identity: (app, store, state_key, snapshot_epoch) + SHA-256 of canonical value.
user_space_classes.content_checksum: SHA-256 over the canonical serialized state value.
user_space_classes.metadata_checksum: SHA-256 over {app, store, state_key, snapshot_epoch, source_file}.
user_space_classes.checkpoint: Cursor = (store, last state_key); snapshot_epoch pins consistency; skip-set = committed value SHA-256.
user_space_classes.exactly_once: Idempotent upsert keyed on (store, state_key, snapshot_epoch); redb runtime classes (PGREDB-001/002) are inventoried, not reclassified.
user_space_classes.failure_recovery: Re-open the pinned snapshot; redb ACID and envctl replay re-derive state; PostgreSQL PITR covers committed staging.
user_space_classes.lossless_reconstruction_test: Reconstruct the state map from staged rows and assert value SHA-256 parity against the snapshot; for redb-durable classes, blobstore_parity SHA-256 symmetry is the reconstruction proof.
user_space_classes.store_role: redb buffer -> PostgreSQL staging app-state table; existing durable redb classes remain owned by redb until a verified STORE-001 cutover.
grounding: STORE-001 transient class + PGREDB-001/002 (durable redb owners, not silently reclassified)
CLAUDE.md storage layer (lifeos.db) and persistence whitelist

user_space_classes.description: Non-text and large-binary assets: images, audio, video, archives, and any opaque blob; represented as a reference plus non-text metadata, with bytes content-addressed.

bounded_collector.surface: Files classified as binary/non-text or exceeding the streaming byte cap of USI-C01.
bounded_collector.bounds: Bytes never decoded/transformed; only measured and hashed; sidecar metadata (MIME, dimensions where cheaply detectable) recorded without decoding content.
bounded_collector.method: Read-only streamed hash + non-text metadata sidecar; bytes handed to the BlobStore.

user_space_classes.exclusion_policy: Skip blocked_paths; do not attempt to parse or transcode; do not extract secrets from binary payloads.
user_space_classes.stable_identity: SHA-256 of blob bytes (content address); identity = (content_sha256, logical_path); a blob reference row points at the BlobStore entry.
user_space_classes.content_checksum: SHA-256 over the exact blob bytes (BlobStore identity).
user_space_classes.metadata_checksum: SHA-256 over {mime_type, byte_size, logical_path, mode, mtime} (non-text metadata).
user_space_classes.ordering_contract: Logical path order; ties by content_sha256.
user_space_classes.checkpoint: Cursor = last committed logical_path; skip-set = committed blob content_sha256 (large-blob-safe resume).
user_space_classes.exactly_once: Content addressing collapses duplicate blobs to one BlobStore entry; reference rows dedup on (content_sha256, logical_path).
user_space_classes.failure_recovery: Re-hash from read-only source; BlobStore SHA-256 identity makes partial transfers self-correcting (blobstore_parity).
user_space_classes.lossless_reconstruction_test: Materialize the blob from the BlobStore by content_sha256 and assert byte-identical to the archived blob; metadata sidecar round-trips exactly.
user_space_classes.store_role: redb buffer / BlobStore (SHA-256) -> PostgreSQL staging blob-reference rows; bytes in the content-addressed BlobStore (PgStore parity target).
grounding: ADOPT-001 BlobStore / SHA-256 identity / exact-byte materialization

user_space_classes.description: Principals and entity identity keys: OS accounts, LifeOS accounts (accounts table), git author identities, and the minted stable identities that bind every collected entity.

bounded_collector.surface: accounts table / account.json, git author metadata, OS ownership (uid/gid) referenced by permissions.
bounded_collector.bounds: References and non-secret attributes only; identity-key minting contract for all classes lives here.
bounded_collector.method: Read-only extraction of principals + deterministic identity-URN minting for entities.

user_space_classes.exclusion_policy: Never collect credentials, keyring material, or private keys; store principal references and public identity attributes only (STORE-001 external-secret class).
user_space_classes.stable_identity: Principal URN = (identity_domain, natural_key) e.g. os-account:uid, lifeos-account:id, git-author:email; entity identities = content-addressed keys minted per class.
user_space_classes.content_checksum: SHA-256 over the canonical principal attribute tuple.
user_space_classes.checkpoint: Cursor = last committed principal URN; skip-set = committed principal SHA-256.
user_space_classes.exactly_once: Idempotent upsert keyed on principal URN; identity minting is a pure function of content so it is inherently idempotent.
user_space_classes.failure_recovery: Re-derive principals from the read-only account/state snapshot; identity minting is deterministic and replayable.
user_space_classes.lossless_reconstruction_test: Reconstruct the principal set and assert every collected entity's foreign key resolves to exactly one principal URN with matching attribute SHA-256; no orphan identities.
user_space_classes.store_role: redb buffer -> PostgreSQL staging identity table; the durable identity anchor for USI-C10/C11/C12 foreign keys.
grounding: CLAUDE.md storage layer accounts migration (account.json -> accounts table)

user_space_classes.description: Access and ownership metadata: POSIX mode bits, uid/gid ownership, and ACLs attached to files and directories.

bounded_collector.surface: stat() metadata for every USI-C01 file and USI-C02 directory, plus ACL entries where present.
bounded_collector.bounds: Metadata only; no permission is changed on any source (read-only stat).
bounded_collector.method: Read-only stat/getfacl emitting a permission tuple per entity.

user_space_classes.exclusion_policy: Skip entities excluded upstream; do not attempt privilege escalation to read protected metadata (record access_denied provenance instead of failing silently).
user_space_classes.stable_identity: (entity_identity, permission_scheme) where entity_identity is the USI-C01/C02 identity and scheme in {posix, acl}.
user_space_classes.content_checksum: SHA-256 over the canonical permission tuple {mode, uid, gid, acl_entries}.
user_space_classes.ordering_contract: Entity order inherited from USI-C01/C02; scheme order posix before acl.
user_space_classes.checkpoint: Cursor = last committed entity_identity; skip-set = committed permission SHA-256.
user_space_classes.failure_recovery: Re-stat from read-only source; permission tuples are deterministic.
user_space_classes.lossless_reconstruction_test: Reconstruct the permission tuple per entity and assert it equals the archived stat tuple (PRESERVE-001 permission capture); reprojected files carry identical mode/owner (POSTGRES-008 permissions parity).
user_space_classes.store_role: redb buffer -> PostgreSQL staging permission table, foreign-keyed to USI-C09 principals and USI-C01/C02 entities.

user_space_classes.description: Temporal and version history: git commit graphs, refs, reflogs, worktrees, and filesystem timestamps — the time dimension of the user-space.

bounded_collector.surface: Git repositories (all refs, branches, reflogs, worktrees) plus file mtime/ctime; git bundle as the archive form.
bounded_collector.bounds: Read-only; captured once per repo as an all-refs bundle (PRESERVE-001), avoiding per-object duplication with USI-C01.
bounded_collector.method: git bundle create --all + rev-list enumeration + reflog/worktree/status/fsck capture.

user_space_classes.exclusion_policy: Do not rewrite or gc history; do not prune reflogs; secrets committed historically are flagged as references, not re-extracted as values.
user_space_classes.stable_identity: git object id (commit/tree/blob oid — already content-addressed by git); repo identity = origin URL + HEAD oid.
user_space_classes.content_checksum: SHA-256 of the git bundle (and native oid per object); the bundle is the byte-exact history archive.
user_space_classes.metadata_checksum: SHA-256 over {repo_identity, ref_map, reflog_digest, worktree_list, fsck_status}.
user_space_classes.ordering_contract: Git topological order (parent commit before child); refs in lexicographic order.
user_space_classes.checkpoint: Cursor = last committed commit oid in topo order; skip-set = committed oids (git is inherently content-addressed).
user_space_classes.exactly_once: Object oids dedup natively; re-bundling an unchanged repo yields the same oid set (idempotent).
user_space_classes.failure_recovery: Restore from the archived bundle + recorded commit id (PRESERVE-001 restore: 'recreate a branch only from the archived bundle and recorded commit ID').
user_space_classes.lossless_reconstruction_test: Clone/verify the bundle and assert every archived ref resolves to its recorded oid and git fsck is clean; full history round-trips.
user_space_classes.store_role: redb buffer / bundle archive -> PostgreSQL staging history tables (commit graph, refs) with oids as identity; PostgreSQL PITR covers committed staging.
grounding: PRESERVE-001 (git bundle, HEAD/branches/reflogs/worktrees/fsck; restore from bundle)

user_space_classes.description: The graph edges that connect entities: directory containment, code import/dependency edges, configuration references, and provenance links — the knowledge-graph fabric.

bounded_collector.surface: Derived during collection from USI-C02 containment, USI-C03 AST dependencies, USI-C04 config references, and provenance of every staged record.
bounded_collector.bounds: Edges reference only already-collected entity identities; dangling edges are rejected (referential integrity).
bounded_collector.method: Deterministic edge extraction keyed on (src_identity, edge_type, dst_identity).

user_space_classes.exclusion_policy: No edge is minted to an excluded/secret entity; provenance edges never carry secret values.
user_space_classes.stable_identity: SHA-256 of the canonical triple (src_identity, edge_type, dst_identity); edge_type in {contains, imports, depends_on, references, derived_from, provenance_of}.
user_space_classes.content_checksum: SHA-256 over the canonical edge triple + attributes.
user_space_classes.metadata_checksum: SHA-256 over {edge_type, src_class, dst_class, extracted_by_collector, run_id}.
user_space_classes.ordering_contract: (src_identity, edge_type, dst_identity) ascending — total order over triples.
user_space_classes.checkpoint: Cursor = last committed edge triple hash; skip-set = committed edge SHA-256.
user_space_classes.exactly_once: Idempotent upsert keyed on the triple hash; a given relationship is minted exactly once.
user_space_classes.failure_recovery: Edges are pure functions of the collected entities; re-derive deterministically after a crash.
user_space_classes.lossless_reconstruction_test: Rebuild the graph from staged edges and assert containment reconstructs the USI-C02 tree and imports reconstruct the USI-C03 dependency set; no edge references a missing entity (Causal Dependency Edges intact).
user_space_classes.store_role: redb buffer -> PostgreSQL staging edge table (feeds RuVector GNN causal edges); vector/graph semantics deferred to POSTGRES-005.
grounding: Blueprint (Causal Dependency Edges, knowledge graph, module_path dependencies)
context matrix (graph structures / GNN causal edges)

user_space_classes.description: Application-domain structured records not covered by the physical classes: LifeOS content items, notes/journal/task entries, media catalog rows, and other typed domain rows.

bounded_collector.surface: Structured domain stores (LifeOS data layer records, app databases, typed record files).
bounded_collector.bounds: Typed row extraction per domain schema; unknown domains staged as raw records (USI-C01) with a domain_unclassified flag (never dropped).
bounded_collector.method: Read-only typed extraction keyed on domain natural key.

user_space_classes.exclusion_policy: Redact secret fields to references; skip ephemeral/derived caches that are regenerable from other classes.
user_space_classes.stable_identity: (domain, record_type, natural_key) + SHA-256 of the canonical record.
user_space_classes.content_checksum: SHA-256 over the canonical serialized domain record.
user_space_classes.metadata_checksum: SHA-256 over {domain, record_type, natural_key, schema_version, source_store}.
user_space_classes.checkpoint: Cursor = (domain, record_type, last natural_key); skip-set = committed record SHA-256.
user_space_classes.exactly_once: Idempotent upsert keyed on (domain, record_type, natural_key, content_sha256).
user_space_classes.failure_recovery: Re-read from the read-only domain store; content addressing makes partial runs resumable.
user_space_classes.lossless_reconstruction_test: Project staged domain rows back to their source shape and assert per-record SHA-256 and ordering match the archived store.
user_space_classes.store_role: redb buffer -> PostgreSQL staging domain tables.

staging_destination.buffer: redb transient staging buffer (STORE-001 transient tier) — first landing zone for all collectors; never the durable authority for a PostgreSQL-owned class.
staging_destination.durable_staging: PostgreSQL staging tables committed by envctl (forward-only bridge); BlobStore holds content-addressed bytes under SHA-256.
staging_destination.no_cutover_here: Staging is pre-authority. The authority switch and retirement of flat files as editable source is POSTGRES-010 under the STORE-001 verified-cutover invariant; nothing here reclassifies a durable redb class (PGREDB-001/002).

provenance_model.grounding: ADOPT-001 provenance (source commit/PR per capability); STORE-001 invariant 'Provenance preserved'.

checkpoint_protocol.store: envctl migration_db (targets, runs, run_events, approvals, checkpoints, rollbacks — PGREDB-002).
checkpoint_protocol.cursor: Per-class monotonic cursor + resume skip-set of committed identities (PGREDB-001 resume skip-set; ADOPT-001 resumable capture).
checkpoint_protocol.restart: An interrupted run resumes from the last committed checkpoint with no re-collection of committed identities and no gaps.

exactly_once_semantics.mechanism: Content-addressed SHA-256 identity + idempotent upsert keyed on identity + BLAKE3 dedup cache (skip recompute) + resume skip-set (skip re-collection).
exactly_once_semantics.result: Any number of retries produces exactly one canonical staged record per source entity.

lossless_reconstruction_test_global.oracle: The archive-first checksummed snapshot (PRESERVE-001 pattern) is the reconstruction oracle.
lossless_reconstruction_test_global.procedure: For every class, envctl projects staged records to a scratch target and asserts content_sha256 parity, metadata tuple equality, and declared ordering against the archive; code classes additionally assert AST-concatenation byte-exact file reconstruction via module_path.
lossless_reconstruction_test_global.pass_condition: Zero byte-level, metadata-level, or ordering-level differences across all classes before any authority claim (feeds POSTGRES-009/010 round-trip proofs).
lossless_reconstruction_test_global.grounding: ADOPT-001 exact-byte materialization; blobstore_parity SHA-256 symmetry; POSTGRES-008 byte-identical projection.

gate_coverage.gate: Every user-space class has a bounded collector, exclusion policy, stable identity, content and metadata checksum, ordering contract, checkpoint, exactly-once behavior, failure recovery, and lossless reconstruction test.
